brief_document.title: Shard router: hash-based key-to-shard mapping
brief_document.purpose: Deliver the ShardRouter — the component that maps arbitrary byte keys to the correct shard actor PID. The design (ADR-004) specifies hash-based routing: shard(K) = BLAKE3(K) % N, where N is fixed at database creation and immutable thereafter. Without a router, the shard actors built in CORE-007 are isolated processes with no way for the database handle (CORE-009) or callers to locate the shard that owns a given key. The router is intentionally minimal: it holds a Vec<beamr::Pid> indexed by shard number and provides both a direct lookup (for callers that want to send themselves) and a forward path (for callers that want to delegate routing). Using BLAKE3 for routing keeps the hash function consistent with the content-addressing used throughout the tree, avoiding a second hashing dependency.
brief_document.task: Create crates/haematite/src/shard/router.rs. Define a ShardRouter struct that holds a Vec<beamr::Pid> of shard actor PIDs (one per shard, indexed by shard number) and a shard_count: usize. Implement ShardRouter::new(pids: Vec<beamr::Pid>) which captures the PID list and derives shard_count from its length. Implement two routing APIs: ShardRouter::shard_for(key: &[u8]) -> usize which returns the shard index (BLAKE3(key) as u64 % shard_count), and ShardRouter::pid_for(key: &[u8]) -> beamr::Pid which returns the PID of the shard actor that owns the key. The shard count is immutable after construction — ShardRouter provides no method to add, remove, or replace PIDs (ADR-004). Wire the new file into shard/mod.rs (which already exists from CORE-007) and re-export ShardRouter from the crate root via lib.rs. This brief does NOT implement the Database handle that constructs the router — that is CORE-009. It does NOT implement read replicas or rebalancing.

requirements.title: ShardRouter struct with immutable PID table
requirements.spec: Define a ShardRouter struct that holds pids: Vec<beamr::Pid> and shard_count: usize. Implement ShardRouter::new(pids: Vec<beamr::Pid>) -> Result<ShardRouter, RouterError> which returns an error if pids is empty, otherwise constructs the router with shard_count set to pids.len(). Define a RouterError type that implements std::error::Error and Debug. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT expose pids or shard_count as mutable fields or provide any method that adds, removes, or replaces entries after construction — shard count is immutable after database creation (ADR-004, C34). ShardRouter SHALL implement Debug.
acceptance: ShardRouter::new(vec![]) returns Err(RouterError) — an empty PID list is rejected
ShardRouter::new(pids) with a non-empty pids slice returns Ok(ShardRouter) with shard_count == pids.len()
ShardRouter implements Debug
RouterError implements std::error::Error
ShardRouter exposes no pub mut fields, no add_shard, remove_shard, or replace_pid methods
cargo check -p haematite succeeds with ShardRouter defined

requirements.title: BLAKE3 key-to-shard index mapping
requirements.spec: WHEN ShardRouter::shard_for(key) is called with a byte-slice key, THE SYSTEM SHALL compute the BLAKE3 hash of the key bytes, interpret the first 8 bytes of the hash output as a little-endian u64, and return (hash_u64 % shard_count) as the shard index. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use any other hash function — BLAKE3 is the project-wide content-addressing function (ADR-002). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT use the key's length, byte sum, or any non-cryptographic function in place of BLAKE3. THE SYSTEM SHALL return a value in [0, shard_count) for any non-empty key.
acceptance: ShardRouter::shard_for(key) returns a value in [0, shard_count) for any key
ShardRouter::shard_for(key) is deterministic: the same key and the same shard_count always produce the same index
ShardRouter::shard_for(b"a") and ShardRouter::shard_for(b"b") produce the same index only by coincidence of the modulus — not structurally
A test with shard_count=256 and 1000 random keys confirms all 256 shards receive at least one key (uniform distribution)
The implementation uses the blake3 crate, not a custom hash — confirmed by grep for blake3::hash in router.rs

requirements.title: PID lookup API for direct shard sends
requirements.spec: WHEN ShardRouter::pid_for(key) is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL return a clone of the beamr::Pid at index shard_for(key) in the internal PID table. This gives callers a direct handle to the shard actor that owns the key, enabling them to send messages without going through the router each time. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT return a PID outside the table — the returned index is always in [0, shard_count) so the Vec index is always valid. ShardRouter SHALL also expose shard_count(&self) -> usize returning the fixed shard count.
acceptance: ShardRouter::pid_for(key) returns the same Pid as pids[shard_for(key)]
ShardRouter::pid_for(key) is deterministic: the same key returns the same Pid
ShardRouter::shard_count() returns pids.len() as passed to ShardRouter::new
pid_for never panics for any non-empty key — the Vec index is always in bounds
A test constructs a router with 4 known Pids, asserts pid_for routes distinct keys to the correct Pid by comparing with shard_for

requirements.title: Wire ShardRouter into shard module and crate root
requirements.spec: Add pub mod router and pub use router::{ShardRouter, RouterError} to crates/haematite/src/shard/mod.rs. Add pub use shard::{ShardRouter, RouterError} to crates/haematite/src/lib.rs alongside the existing shard re-exports. shard/mod.rs SHALL NOT contain any logic beyond pub mod and pub use declarations (CN3). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT introduce a new mod.rs or alter the module hierarchy — shard/mod.rs already exists from CORE-007 and is extended here.
acceptance: haematite::ShardRouter is accessible from the crate root after wiring
haematite::RouterError is accessible from the crate root after wiring
grep for fn, struct, enum, impl in shard/mod.rs — excluding any pub use lines — returns no matches
cargo check -p haematite succeeds with ShardRouter wired in
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests in shard::router

requirements.title: Routing correctness and uniformity tests
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL include unit tests in crates/haematite/src/shard/router.rs that verify: (a) routing is deterministic across calls, (b) all shard indices are in bounds, (c) key distribution across shards is approximately uniform for random keys, and (d) ShardRouter::new rejects an empty PID list. Tests SHALL NOT use unwrap() or expect() outside #[cfg(test)] blocks. THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT depend on any test-only crate beyond what is already in Cargo.toml.
acceptance: A #[test] asserts shard_for(key) == shard_for(key) for the same key called twice
A #[test] asserts shard_for(key) < shard_count for 100 distinct keys with shard_count=16
A #[test] constructs a router with 256 shards, routes 2560 random keys, and asserts every shard index in 0..256 appears at least once
A #[test] asserts ShardRouter::new(vec![]) returns Err
cargo test -p haematite --lib shard::router passes with no failures

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the Database handle (CORE-009) that constructs ShardRouter by spawning shard actors — the router accepts pre-spawned Pids
SHALL NOT implement consistent hashing or virtual nodes for resharding — ADR-004 explicitly defers dynamic resharding to a future phase
SHALL NOT implement read replicas or secondary shard processes — deferred to a future phase
SHALL NOT implement any message forwarding logic inside the router — routing is a lookup, not a proxy; callers send directly using pid_for
SHALL NOT define ShardActor or any shard actor message handling — those live in shard/actor.rs (CORE-007)
verification: cargo check -p haematite succeeds with no errors
cargo test -p haematite --lib passes all tests in the shard::router module
cargo clippy -p haematite -- -D warnings produces no warnings
grep -rn 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/haematite/src/shard/router.rs produces no matches outside #[cfg(test)] blocks
grep -n '.' crates/haematite/src/shard/mod.rs | grep -v 'pub mod\|pub use\|^[[:space:]]*$\|//' produces no output — mod.rs contains only declarations
haematite::ShardRouter and haematite::RouterError are importable from the crate root in a doc-test or integration test

adrs.title: Actor-per-shard with fixed shard count at creation
adrs.decision: Actor-per-shard on beamr with fixed shard count at creation, over single-writer and lock-per-shard, because the actor model eliminates locks entirely — each shard is a single-writer beamr process with no shared mutable state. Fixed shard count over dynamic resharding because resharding is operationally complex and can be deferred: 256 or 1024 shards covers any reasonable single-node deployment for Aion's workload (one key per workflow_id).

checklist.text: ShardRouter maps keys to shard actors via hash(key) % shard_count.
Shard count is fixed at database creation and immutable thereafter (ADR-004).

stories.text: As an application developer, I want to put and get key-value pairs without thinking about which shard owns them so that the sharding is transparent.
As a concurrent writer, I want to write to different keys from hundreds of beamr processes simultaneously without any of them blocking so that write throughput scales linearly with shard count.

constraints.text: No file over 500 lines. If a module approaches this limit, extract into submodules.
No panics in production code. .unwrap() and .expect() only in tests.
mod.rs contains ONLY pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports. No logic.
All public types implement Debug. All error types implement std::error::Error.
The prolly tree root hash for a given key-value set must be deterministic and history-independent: the same keys and values always produce the same root hash regardless of insertion order.
WAL buffer reads must shadow tree reads: a key written to the buffer but not yet committed must be returned by get, not the stale tree value.
Shard actor crash must not affect other shards. Supervision restarts only the failed shard.
Batch append (multiple key-value pairs in one call) must produce exactly one tree commit, not N individual commits.

resolved_context.intention: Haematite's core is a storage engine where concurrency is free and branching is structural. When this cluster is complete, a developer can open a haematite database, write to it from hundreds of concurrent actors without any locking discipline, fork it instantly by sharing a hash, and merge two forks by walking only the nodes that differ. The experience should feel like working with an in-memory data structure that happens to be durable — no connection pools, no transaction coordinators, no lock managers. The content-addressed prolly tree is what makes branching zero-friction; the actor-per-shard model is what makes concurrency zero-contention; the WAL buffer is what makes writes fast despite the content-addressing overhead. All three are load-bearing.
